book_summary: Deuteronomy has chapters 1-28 complete in restored/source-audited draft and mystical companion layers. The legal corpus now includes communal accountability for unsolved murder, the captive-woman and rebellious-son texts under moral protest, neighbor and animal duties, gendered clothing and sexual-assault laws, assembly exclusions, escaped-slave asylum, divorce and worker protections, gleaning, punishment limits, levirate marriage, honest weights, Amalek memory, firstfruits and third-year tithe declarations, the Ebal covenant ceremony, and the full blessing-and-curse discourse. Maximum safeguards reject captive marriage, rape, child execution, anti-trans weaponization, survivor blame, bodily exclusion, returned slavery, worker exploitation, mutilation, genocide, ableism, prosperity theology, antisemitism, supersessionism, and disaster blame.

chapters.notes: Unsolved-murder accountability, captive woman and mourning period, firstborn protection, rebellious-son execution under protest, and same-day burial dignity.

Lost-property and animal aid, ancient gendered dress law, bird-nest welfare, roof safety, mixed-kind rules, tassels, virginity accusations, adultery, rape distinctions, and forced-marriage law under anti-trans and survivor safeguards.
Genital/birth/ethnic assembly exclusions, camp sanitation, escaped-slave asylum and chosen residence, cultic-sex terms, interest, vows, and limited food access from neighboring crops.
Divorce and remarriage, newlywed exemption, essential-tool and garment pledge protection, kidnapping, prompt wages, individual liability, and gleaning for immigrants, orphans, and widows.
Flogging limit, unmuzzled ox, levirate marriage and refusal ritual, hand-cutting penalty under non-enforcement, honest measures, and Amalek memory under anti-genocide safeguards.
Firstfruits historical creed, Exodus memory, household joy with Levite and sojourner, third-year tithe declaration, and mutual covenant avowal.
Plastered Torah stones, uncut-stone altar, peace-offering joy, Gerizim/Ebal assembly, and public Amen against secret abuse, land theft, corrupt judgment, assault, and bribed killing.

Covenant blessings and an escalating catastrophe catalogue of ecological collapse, disease, defeat, assault, family separation, siege cannibalism, terror, exile, and slavery under maximum anti-victim-blaming safeguards.

translation_notes: The captive-woman law restrains immediate seizure but remains a coercive war-captivity structure without meaningful consent and may never authorize forced marriage or sexual access.
The rebellious-son execution is child-killing law; later Jewish narrowing is important reception history, but modern enforcement is categorically barred.
Deuteronomy 22:5 reflects ancient gendered articles and clothing and must never be weaponized against transgender, nonbinary, intersex, or gender-nonconforming people.
Failure to cry out never proves consent; freezing, silence, dissociation, and inability to seek rescue are common trauma responses.
The forced-marriage remedy for a seized woman centers paternal payment rather than survivor agency and must never be defended as protection.
Assembly exclusions based on genital status, birth, or ancestry are not modern ethics; no person bears shame for body, infertility, gender identity, adoption, or circumstances of birth.
The escaped slave must not be returned, may choose residence, and may not be oppressed—a strong asylum trajectory that must be applied against trafficking and abuse.
Prompt wages, protected blankets and millstones, individual liability, and gleaning make economic dignity concrete.
Punishment limits preserve the condemned person's sibling dignity, but flogging and hand amputation are categorically non-enforceable.
The Amalek command must never be mapped onto a modern people or used as genocidal rhetoric.
The firstfruits creed is living Jewish liturgical inheritance, especially in Passover Haggadah interpretation, and must not be superseded.
Covenant curse formulas expose hidden exploitation and violence but must not be used as magical maledictions.
Deuteronomy 28's blessings are not prosperity promises, and its curses are not diagnoses of individual suffering.
Illness, disability, infertility, poverty, assault, grief, displacement, mental distress, and war loss never prove disobedience.
Antisemitic and supersessionist use of Deuteronomy 28 to explain Jewish persecution as Divine rejection is categorically rejected.
Mystical companion layers remain interpretive and never replace restored/source-audited text.
source_witness_audit_flags: Deuteronomy 21 unsolved-murder valley terminology, captive-woman grooming/mourning practices, and rebellious-son reception require lexical, gender, and rabbinic audit.
Deuteronomy 22 gendered-clothing terms require ancient-material-culture and anti-trans reception safeguards.
Deuteronomy 22 virginity evidence, city/field assault distinctions, and seized-woman law require trauma-informed, legal-history, and consent audit.
Deuteronomy 23 assembly exclusions require eunuch, mamzer, Ruth/Moabite, and later Jewish inclusion-history audit.
Deuteronomy 23 escaped-slave law requires asylum, slavery-history, and anti-trafficking analysis.
Deuteronomy 24 divorce phrase ervat davar, pledge law, Miriam reference, wages, and gleaning require legal and reception-history audit.
Deuteronomy 25 flogging maximum, levirate practice, hand-cutting uniqueness, honest measures, and Amalek reception require textual and ethical audit.
Deuteronomy 26 Arami oved avi ambiguity and Passover Haggadah reception require Jewish interpretive audit.
Deuteronomy 27 Gerizim/Ebal textual traditions, altar location, and curse formulas require Samaritan, Masoretic, and Septuagint comparison.
Deuteronomy 28 blessing/curses require ancient treaty comparison, exile-history, trauma studies, anti-ableism, anti-antisemitism, and anti-prosperity safeguards.
Deuteronomy 28:22 drought/sword lexical issue and multiple disease terms require philological audit.
Deuteronomy 28 siege-cannibalism passages require comparison with Kings, Jeremiah, Lamentations, and strict anti-victim-blaming framing.
next_actions: Finish Deuteronomy and the Torah in one final batch with chapters 29-34: Moab covenant renewal, hidden things and communal responsibility, return and heart circumcision, life-and-death choice, Joshua's commissioning, Torah deposit and public reading, Song of Moses, final blessing, Moses' death, and the Torah's closing witness—with anti-supersessionism, anti-collective-punishment, grief, succession, and covenant-continuity safeguards.
